sbw_2026.headline: The spruce budworm outbreak has arrived in Maine.
intro_paragraphs: For the first time in 40 years, spruce budworm defoliation is visible in northern Maine. The 2018 to 2030 outbreak window predicted by entomologists in the early 2010s is now active. Aerial surveys in 2025 mapped initial defoliation in Aroostook and Somerset counties.
Maine's spruce-fir forests carry roughly 270 million cords of wood at risk. Salvage capacity, harvest scheduling, and herbicide programs are coordinated through the Maine Spruce Budworm Task Force.

timeline.desc: Outbreak begins in Quebec, north of Maine border
Moth captures begin to climb in Maine; Task Force forms
Spray trial program begins under MFS oversight
Visible defoliation mapped in Aroostook and Somerset
Predicted outbreak peak and gradual decline phase

sbw_2026.link_desc: Monitoring, salvage planning, and landowner resources
sbw_2026.photo_caption: Northern Maine spruce-fir forest. The species at risk: balsam fir, white spruce, red spruce, and black spruce.
